purpose: Custom resize hook for the bottom-right-anchored Claude floating panel. Uses pointer events (NOT mouse events), pointer capture, and requestAnimationFrame to drive 60fps resize via direct element.style mutations — never re-renders. Listens for pointerdown on elements with data-resize-edge attribute inside a panel ref, then tracks pointermove/pointerup on document. Supports three edges: 'left' (width), 'top' (height), 'top-left' (both). Honors minWidth/minHeight/maxHeight options.

exports.name: useResize
exports.signature: useResize(panelRef, viewMode, options?: { minWidth, minHeight, maxHeight })

gotchas: Only active when viewMode === 'expanded' — minimized/collapsed/maximized modes get cleanupDrag() called and the hook returns early without attaching listeners.
Direct DOM mutation (panel.style.width = ...) — React is bypassed entirely. Re-rendering the panel will reset style unless caller restores via onResizeStop or persistence.
Pointer capture (e.target.setPointerCapture) keeps events flowing even when the cursor leaves the browser window — important for fullscreen drags.
ClaudeFloatingPanel in App.jsx ALSO has its own legacy mousedown drag handler — useResize coexists with it; both must be kept in sync if you change resize behavior.
rAF cancellation: only the latest pointermove rAF is kept (cancelAnimationFrame on each new event) — drag stays smooth at high pointer rates.
Body cursor and userSelect are temporarily overridden during drag — cleanupDrag MUST run on pointerup OR effect teardown OR the page is left with broken cursor/selection state.
minWidth (360) / minHeight (300) / maxHeight (800) defaults are hardcoded — changing here doesn't affect the legacy in-component drag.
patterns: Direct style.* mutation inside rAF for drag/resize — avoids React re-render thrashing and keeps drags at native frame rate.
Pointer events + setPointerCapture is more robust than mouse events for window-leaving drags — use this pattern for any custom drag UI.
data-resize-edge attribute selector for delegated handle detection — handles are siblings inside the panel; click on any handle reads its edge from dataset.
conventions: Hook stores mutable drag state in useRef (never useState) — drag state is not part of render.
useCallback for cleanupDrag so it can be referenced from both pointerup and effect teardown without creating closures with stale refs.
